title: ai-infra-engineer-learning project specifications
description: Canonical project specifications for the AI Infrastructure Engineer learning track. Each project README cites the spec ID below (e.g. proj-101, proj-102, proj-103). Treat this file as the source of truth for project scope, deliverables, and acceptance criteria.

projects.title: Basic Model Serving with FastAPI + Docker + Kubernetes
projects.summary: Build a production-grade FastAPI inference service for a scikit-learn classifier. Containerize, deploy to Kubernetes with HPA, expose via Ingress, and instrument with Prometheus metrics + Grafana dashboards.
learning_outcomes: Serve an sklearn model behind a typed FastAPI interface with versioned response schema
Author a multi-stage Dockerfile with explicit, pinned base images
Deploy to k8s with resource requests/limits, liveness + readiness probes, and HPA
Add Prometheus metrics (latency histogram, request counter, in-flight gauge) and a Grafana dashboard
Operate the system: rolling update, rollback, scale up/down
deliverables: Dockerfile with multi-stage build and non-root runtime user
Kubernetes manifests (Deployment, Service, Ingress, HPA, ServiceMonitor)
README walking through local + cluster deployment
Tests: pytest unit + integration tests with at least 70% line coverage
acceptance_criteria: POST /predict returns sub-100ms latency at P95 for a single-request load
GET /health and GET /ready respond correctly under all observed states (cold start, ready, model load failure)
Service scales from 1 to 5 replicas under load via HPA
Grafana dashboard renders all four golden signals

projects.title: End-to-End MLOps Pipeline with MLflow + DVC + Airflow
prerequisites: proj-101 complete (or equivalent prior FastAPI + K8s deploy experience)
Familiarity with Python ML training workflows
projects.summary: Build an end-to-end MLOps pipeline. Data ingestion via Airflow, feature engineering with versioned artifacts via DVC, experiment tracking + model registry via MLflow, automated retraining triggered by drift detection, and a deployment step that promotes registry models to the serving cluster.
learning_outcomes: Author an Airflow DAG that ingests, validates, and transforms data with retry + alerting semantics
Use DVC to version both data and model artifacts in a way that survives team handoffs
Track experiments in MLflow with consistent naming, tagging, and parameter/metric conventions
Promote model versions through Staging → Production with explicit governance gates
Implement drift detection that triggers retraining automatically when feature distributions shift
deliverables: MLflow tracking server deployment (docker-compose for local, K8s for prod)
Model promotion workflow with audit log
acceptance_criteria: Airflow DAG runs end-to-end without manual intervention
DVC reproduces a prior model run byte-for-byte from a tagged commit
MLflow registry shows at least 3 model versions with clear stage transitions
Drift detection correctly flags a synthetic shift (concept drift inserted manually)
Promotion blocked by a failing gate (e.g., accuracy regression on holdout set)
rubric_dimensions: Reproducibility (can a colleague rerun a prior model from a tag)
Tracking discipline (naming conventions, tagging, artifact completeness)

projects.title: Production LLM Inference Service with Optimization
prerequisites: proj-101 + proj-102 complete (or equivalent serving + MLOps experience)
Access to at least one A10G / A100 / H100 / L40S GPU instance (cloud or local)
projects.summary: Deploy an open-source LLM (Llama-3.1 or Mistral family) behind a production HTTP+SSE API with continuous batching, PagedAttention, prefix caching, FP8 / AWQ quantization, streaming, prometheus metrics, and rigorous load + cost analysis.
learning_outcomes: Choose an inference framework (vLLM vs TGI vs Triton) with explicit trade-off reasoning
Configure continuous batching + PagedAttention + prefix caching for the workload mix
Apply quantization (AWQ, GPTQ, or FP8) and measure the accuracy/latency/throughput trade-off
Build a streaming SSE API with backpressure, request cancellation, and per-tenant rate limits
Run a load test and produce a cost-per-million-tokens breakdown across hardware tiers
deliverables: Working serving stack (docker-compose for local dev, Helm chart for K8s)
Prometheus metrics covering queue depth, time-to-first-token, inter-token latency, GPU mem usage
Grafana dashboard tailored to LLM serving
Load test harness (k6 or locust) with reproducible scenarios
Cost analysis spreadsheet (or doc) comparing at least 3 hardware options
acceptance_criteria: Sustain ≥ 50 concurrent streams with TTFT < 500ms P95 on the target GPU
Quantized variant within 1.5pp of baseline on a held-out evaluation set
Streaming API supports client cancellation (server stops generating within 100ms)
Grafana dashboard surfaces queue saturation and KV cache pressure correctly
Documented cost-per-million-tokens within 15% of measured production cost

schema_notes.id: Stable identifier referenced from project README files.
schema_notes.slug: Matches the directory name under projects/.
schema_notes.duration_hours: Estimated focused work hours, including reading + implementation + write-up.
schema_notes.difficulty: One of: easy, medium, medium-high, high, advanced.
schema_notes.rubric_dimensions: High-level grading axes; the project's own rubric.md (if present) provides per-level descriptors.
